tools.title: American Epidemic Timeline
tools.summary: A cinematic, source-first timeline of major U.S.-linked epidemics and disease outbreaks, built as an expandable public-health memory machine rather than a fake exhaustive list.
tools.evidence_model: Curated outbreak ledger with source links, rights-checked archival visuals, uncertainty notes, and public-health turning points
tools.long_note: The timeline launches with a major-and-expandable canon of U.S.-linked outbreaks, then grows through a structured event ledger as more local and regional events are verified.

tools.title: Pathogen Atlas
tools.summary: A curated origin-and-spread atlas that links geography, uncertainty, papers, and prior writing instead of flattening pathogens into another story stream.
tools.evidence_model: Curated origin claims, route confidence, and linked papers
tools.long_note: The pathogen atlas runs as a dedicated dark atlas app while drawing from the curated epi-dossier atlas export.

tools.title: Maritime Disease Atlas
tools.summary: A darker, map-forward atlas of shipboard disease ecology, ports, vectors, and the movement constraints of life at sea.
tools.evidence_model: Curated map scenarios with route, mechanism, and historical narrative
tools.long_note: This is the main visual precedent for the atlas family and stays closer to a control-room cartographic interface than a standard article page.

tools.title: Revolutionary War Disease Atlas
tools.summary: A historical epidemiology section focused on colonial and Revolutionary-era disease ecology, troop movement, and the public-health constraints around the founding period.
tools.evidence_model: Essay-linked historical synthesis with future map promotion
tools.long_note: This section launches as a structured historical atlas page with linked essays first, then graduates into a fuller interactive once the map layer is ready.

tools.title: Viking Health Atlas
tools.summary: A Norse-world disease and health map that ties Greenland, Vinland, settlement ecology, and pre-Columbian contact arguments into one visual surface.
tools.evidence_model: Prototype map plus linked historical essays
tools.long_note: The current Viking build is strong enough to surface publicly as a prototype atlas while the surrounding essay and evidence layer fills in.
